ticket.subject: Welcome to Shopify Partner PluginHive! We’re here to help you save both time and money
ticket.raw_subject: Welcome to Shopify Partner PluginHive! We’re here to help you save both time and money
ticket.description:
Hello Little,

 Greeting from Shopify Partner PluginHive!

 Thank you for installing our Shopify App - **“PH Multi Carrier Shipping Label App”** on xtbtgx-hi.myshopify.com

 Our team would like to assist you with the complete app onboarding.

 We request you let us know a suitable time for the onboarding session, which will not take more than 10 mins. Feel free to reply to this email (or) share your contact number, we will get in touch with you.

 You can also refer our documentation to set up the app and start shipping - click here to know more

 Looking forward to hearing from you.

 Thanks and Regards,
 Team PluginHive
https://www.pluginhive.com/about/#team

comments.body:
USA Store
99 orders

UPS account connected

Explored app for rates but no action in labels

Call Summary:
The customer mentioned that they expect to process around 700–1000 orders per month.

Their primary requirement is multi-package shipments, where a few items from an order are packed into individual packages. During the call, we discussed the packaging settings and updating product details, and the customer confirmed that they understood the configuration.

We also reviewed the order processing workflow. During the call, we attempted to generate labels for a few orders, and the label generation worked smoothly.

However, the customer’s requirement is to print the product names on the shipping label as a reference. Currently, the app provides an option to print the order number as a reference, but not the product names.

The customer mentioned that on UPS.com, they were able to generate labels with the product name included as a reference, and they shared a sample label for reference.

At this stage, the customer indicated that they would prefer not to proceed with the app unless the product names can be printed on the labels.
comments.html_body:
USA Store
99 orders
 
UPS account connected
 
Explored app for rates but no action in labels
 
Call Summary:
The customer mentioned that they expect to process around 700–1000 orders per month.
 
Their primary requirement is multi-package shipments, where a few items from an order are packed into individual packages. During the call, we discussed the packaging settings and updating product details, and the customer confirmed that they understood the configuration.
 
We also reviewed the order processing workflow. During the call, we attempted to generate labels for a few orders, and the label generation worked smoothly.
 
However, the customer’s requirement is to print the product names on the shipping label as a reference. Currently, the app provides an option to print the order number as a reference, but not the product names.
 
The customer mentioned that on UPS.com, they were able to generate labels with the product name included as a reference, and they shared a sample label for reference.
 
At this stage, the customer indicated that they would prefer not to proceed with the app unless the product names can be printed on the labels.

comments.body:
Hello Diane,

Thank you for your time and patience while we review this case.

We tested the scenario and confirmed that the app is passing the product name as the description in the request. The description is currently being printed on international labels, but it does not appear on domestic labels.

I have attached both the domestic and international labels, along with the label request responses. Could you please share these files with your UPS account representative and check if any changes are required in label request to print the product description on domestic UPS labels?

Please let us know how it goes or if you need any additional assistance or information from our end.

Thanks and Regards,
Manohar V
Team PluginHive
comments.html_body:
Hello Diane,
 
Thank you for your time and patience while we review this case.
 
We tested the scenario and confirmed that the app is passing the product name as the description in the request. The description is currently being printed on international labels, but it does not appear on domestic labels.
 
I have attached both the domestic and international labels, along with the label request responses. Could you please share these files with your UPS account representative and check if any changes are required in label request to print the product description on domestic UPS labels?
 
Please let us know how it goes or if you need any additional assistance or information from our end.

Thanks and Regards,
Manohar V
Team PluginHive

comments.body:
Hi Sibi,



We spoke with UPS. Where we type the product name is in the UPS field "Reference 1" (and sometimes "Reference 2"). We have been able to do do this for 14 years and it works beautifully for us, so we're trying to accomplish this while using your App 🙂 So we spoke with UPS and shared your email below, UPS said the following:



"The Reference number container needs to be declared in the package service option container, instead of the shipment service options container." They think you're using the Reference field in the shipment service option container, and that's why it's not showing up on the label. Would you please share this with your team and see if you are able to get a label out with the Reference 1 field on the domestic label?



Thank you so much for your help.



Best,



Diane



Little Sprout

(877)452-2297

support@littlesproutusa.com

LittleSproutUSA.com

comments.html_body:
Request #380893 "Re: C-0357182449 - Print Referen..." was closed and merged into this request. Last comment in request #380893:

Hi Manohar and PlugInHive Team,

Please see email from the UPS team below, with information regarding the Reference 1 field. This is regarding our current discussion of printing onto the Reference 1 field on the UPS shipment label.

Thank you,

Diane

On Wed, Mar 25, 2026 at 4:20 PM UPS CUSTOMER SERVICE customerservice@ups.com wrote:

Hello Diane,

This is Prakash writing to you from the UPS API support team.

In order to have the REFERNCE NUMBER printed on the UPS label from API, you will need to add the following in your SHIP API call-

{“ShipmentRequest”: {“Shipment”: {“Package”: {“ReferenceNumber”: {}}}}}

          Example-
"Package": {

            "ReferenceNumber": [

                {

                    "Value": "111"

                },

                {

                    "Value": "23"

                }

            ],

SHIP API documentation can be located in the UPS Developer Portal- UPS Developer Portal | UPS - United States

Feel free to reply to this email for API assistance.

Prakash C

UPS Developer API Support

Starting April 1, 2026, the lifespan of OAuth tokens will be reduced from 4 hours to 1 hour. This means your integration must generate a new token every hour instead of every 4 hours to maintain uninterrupted access to UPS APIs. If your integration is hardcoded to refresh tokens every 4 hours, you will need to update your logic.

The information provided by United Parcel Service (UPS) is provided "as is" without warranty of any kind. UPS disclaims all warranties, either express or implied, including the warranties of merchantability and fitness for a particular purpose. In no event shall United Parcel Service, Inc. or its suppliers be liable for any damages whatsoever including direct, indirect, incidental, consequential, loss or business profits or special damages, even if United Parcel Service or its suppliers have been advised of the possibility of such damage. Some states do not allow the exclusion or limitation of liability for consequential or incidental damages so the foregoing limitations may not apply.
© United Parcel Service of America, Inc.

comments.body:
Need assistance from L3 team

**Case:** Request for an option to print the product name on UPS domestic labels.

**Details:**
This is a $29 Popular plan customer who is requesting an option to print the product name as a description on UPS domestic labels. Currently, the product name is printed on international labels but not on domestic labels.

For the customer’s business use case, printing the product name on the label is mandatory. They have mentioned that they may not be able to continue using the app without this feature.

The customer shared a sample label generated via UPS.com, where they have the option to enter a description that gets printed on the label. They have been using this process for the past 14 years. However, they do not want to manually enter the description for each order and are looking to automate this.

As per the discussion, they expect to process around 1000 orders and are open to upgrading to the $49 Premium plan if required to enable this feature.

The customer also contacted UPS support and shared their response below:

* * *
The Reference number container needs to be declared in the package service option container, instead of the shipment service options container.

In order to have the REFERNCE NUMBER printed on the UPS label from API, you will need to add the following in your SHIP API call-
{“ShipmentRequest”: {“Shipment”: {“Package”: {“ReferenceNumber”: {}}}}}


    Example-"Package": { "ReferenceNumber": [{ "Value": "111" }, { "Value": "23" }],SHIP API documentation can be located in the UPS Developer Portal- UPS Developer Portal | UPS - United States

Feel free to reply to this email for API assistance.

* * *
I have attached the following for your review:

- Sample UPS label
- Label generated from the app
- Label request/response files

Could you please review this and share your inputs?

**Store Details:**
Store URL –
Account ID –
comments.html_body:
Need assistance from L3 team
 
Case: Request for an option to print the product name on UPS domestic labels.
 
Details:
This is a $29 Popular plan customer who is requesting an option to print the product name as a description on UPS domestic labels. Currently, the product name is printed on international labels but not on domestic labels.
 
For the customer’s business use case, printing the product name on the label is mandatory. They have mentioned that they may not be able to continue using the app without this feature.
 
The customer shared a sample label generated via UPS.com, where they have the option to enter a description that gets printed on the label. They have been using this process for the past 14 years. However, they do not want to manually enter the description for each order and are looking to automate this.
 
As per the discussion, they expect to process around 1000 orders and are open to upgrading to the $49 Premium plan if required to enable this feature.
 
The customer also contacted UPS support and shared their response below:
The Reference number container needs to be declared in the package service option container, instead of the shipment service options container.
 
In order to have the REFERNCE NUMBER printed on the UPS label from API, you will need to add the following in your SHIP API call-
{“ShipmentRequest”: {“Shipment”: {“Package”: {“ReferenceNumber”: {}}}}}
 
Example-
"Package": {

            "ReferenceNumber": [

                {

                    "Value": "111"

                },

                {

                    "Value": "23"

                }

            ],
SHIP API documentation can be located in the UPS Developer Portal- UPS Developer Portal | UPS - United States

Feel free to reply to this email for API assistance.
I have attached the following for your review:
  • Sample UPS label
  • Label generated from the app
  • Label request/response files
 
Could you please review this and share your inputs?
 
Store Details:
Store URL –
Account ID –

comments.body:
Hello Diane,

Thank you for your patience.

We have checked your request and we will be enhancing the app to print the product description on the UPS domestic labels.

This enhancement will be available in the upcoming release. As of now, we do not have an exact ETA to share with you.

We will reach out to you as soon as the enhancement is made available.

Thanks and Regards,
Touseef
Team PluginHive
comments.html_body:
Hello Diane,
 
Thank you for your patience.
 
We have checked your request and we will be enhancing the app to print the product description on the UPS domestic labels.
 
This enhancement will be available in the upcoming release. As of now, we do not have an exact ETA to share with you.
 
We will reach out to you as soon as the enhancement is made available.
 
Thanks and Regards,
Touseef
Team PluginHive

comments.body:
This case was in dev needed - to enhacne app to print product description in the reference field for UPS Domestic labels

Moving this case to L3 to request an ETA.

**Details:**
The customer has expressed urgency regarding this enhancement. They have been using this field for over 10 years, and it is critical for their warehouse operations, especially for processing large and bulky shipments. They’ve indicated that without the product description on the label, their fulfillment process becomes difficult.

The customer has asked for a timeline so they can decide whether to continue with our solution or explore alternatives.

Could you please provide an ETA for this enhancement so we can update the customer accordingly?
